---
title: Serve
---

*Calepin* has two commands for local development: `preview` and `serve`.

# Preview

`calepin preview` renders the site, starts a local server, and watches for changes. When a `.qmd` file is saved, the page is re-rendered and the browser reloads automatically.

```
calepin preview _calepin.yaml
calepin preview _calepin.yaml -p 4000
calepin preview _calepin.yaml -q
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-p`, `--port` | Server port (default: 3456) |
| `-f`, `--format` | Output format |
| `-s`, `--set` | Override YAML metadata fields |
| `-q`, `--quiet` | Suppress progress messages |

For single files, `calepin preview file.qmd` renders and serves one document with live reload.

# Serve

`calepin serve` is a static file server with no rendering or watching. Use it to serve a pre-built `_site/` directory.

```
calepin serve _site
calepin serve _site -p 8080
```

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-p`, `--port` | Server port (default: 3456) |
| `-q`, `--quiet` | Suppress progress messages |
